headline: Old Spice Teams Up With Iconic R&B Group to Ignite Beloved "Mom Song" in New Campaign: 'The End of Adolescents'
summary: CINCINNATI, March 10, 2026--Old Spice, the iconic brand dedicated to the journey from boyhood to manhood, has launched its revitalized "Mom Song" campaign, 'The End of Adolescents.’ Old Spice licensed the song ‘End of the Road’ by the legendary R&B group, Boyz II Men. The campaign brings back the universally relatable experience of moms grappling with their sons' newfound manliness, a theme that was first introduced by Old Spice over a decade ago in the original, "Mom Song."

content.title: Old Spice Teams Up With Iconic R&B Group to Ignite Beloved "Mom Song" in New Campaign: 'The End of Adolescents'

The campaign brings back the universally relatable experience of moms grappling with their sons' newfound manliness, a theme that was first introduced by Old Spice over a decade ago in the original, "Mom Song."

thumbnail.caption: Old Spice Remixes the Classic R&B ‘Mom Song’ to Launch Swagger Signature Scent Control

headline: Are P&G’s Upgraded Mr. Clean and Gain Lines Quietly Redefining Its Brand Moat (PG)?
summary: In early March 2026, Procter & Gamble expanded its home and fabric care portfolio with major upgrades to Mr. Clean Magic Erasers and Shower & Tub Scrubbers, launched the higher-performance Gain Plus detergents, introduced Native’s Sensitive Series, and unveiled Febreze’s 2026 Scent of the Year, Tranquil Cyprus Coves. Together, these innovations highlight P&G’s push to refine established brands around tougher cleaning performance, sensory appeal, and solutions for sensitive consumers,...

headline: Is It Worth Investing in P&G (PG) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
summary: Based on the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), P&G (PG) should be added to one's portfolio. Wall Street analysts' overly optimistic recommendations cast doubt on the effectiveness of this highly sought-after metric. So, is the stock worth buying?
